focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 4

Eight days after Damian walked out of the arts reception with the Westalian mission address in his pocket, Anya was back in the Foreign Affairs Ministry under another name. Tonight the badge on her jacket said ANNA KESSLER, because this room liked full names and department lines and last names that matched the guest list. A week ago he'd gotten Anya, easy and informal, because the cultural exchange people treated first names like a policy instrument. The state dinner had been a third card for a third room. If anybody from either government ever laid the badges side by side, they'd stop laughing at the bureaucracy and start asking harder questions.

Not tonight. Later was how damage liked to arrive.

The deputy minister had been murdering tariff reform for forty straight minutes when Brandt slid into the empty seat beside Damian and bent his head in a way that looked polite from a distance and predatory from where Anya sat. Most of the audience had the dead-eyed patience of people being held hostage by their own careers, and the abandoned translation headsets on the back table had already knotted themselves into a little plastic nest of surrender. Damian still had his diplomatic posture on, one ankle hooked back under the chair, folder balanced on one knee, but the line of his shoulders had gone half an inch stiffer the second Brandt sat down.

*...if Dreher says framework again I'm going to bite somebody...*

*...don't forget potatoes. He'll complain all through dinner if there aren't potatoes...*

*...who put dill on salmon in October...*

Then Brandt's surface thoughts came through hard enough to lift gooseflesh on Anya's arms.

*...security meeting again. Not on the invite list. Naval attaché Monday, depot office Wednesday, now this. Not random.*

So tha...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya files a misleading report to WISE, downgrading Damian's threat profile and minimizing Renewal Front intelligence
She reads Sylvia's complete trust during the debriefing — the specific pain of watching someone believe your lie while you read the trust that makes it possible
The false report is a chapter-level event, not a transition between scenes — dramatize the weight of this institutional betrayal
Cultural exchange event: Damian's partial recognition — something about her face, her laugh
The dual-layer conversation: spoken words vs. Damian's unfiltered thoughts
Comic irony: Anya managing her reactions to his embarrassing/flattering thoughts
Anya pushing the connection past protocol because of both strategic need and genuine interest
prev_tail_excerpt:
...d for it without meaning to, and that meant the memory was close enough to surface again the next time some stupid detail lined up: a laugh, a gesture, Becky saying the wrong thing at the wrong time if the universe felt theatrical.

The report sat in the machine half-finished.

Anya read it from the top, adjusted one phrase, cut another, and added a softer uncertainty where the truth pressed too hard against the edges. By the end it looked like good analytical caution. That was the part she hated most.

She slid the page out and set it beside the torn remains of the first two versions. The desk was a mess of paper and bad choices: one true and unusable, one cautious and still too dangerous, one smooth enough to survive daylight.

She gathered the third version, clipped it into a plain folder, and left the raw notes loose under her palm for a second too long.

Tomorrow morning she would walk into Sylvia's office with neat paper, controlled voice, and a professional assessment built on scraps, observation, and a lie she had now told enough times inside her own head to make it sound trained.

The first two drafts went into the ashtray on the windowsill. She struck a match, touched flame to one corner, and watched the paper blacken inward. The fire ate Damian's name first. Then Griesler. Then April. The room smelled briefly of smoke and chemical ink.

When the ash cooled, she crushed it flat with the matchbook and rinsed the tray clean.

The folder stayed on the desk.

Anya turned out the lamp, stood in the dark apartment with the ministry still humming at the back of her skull, and understood that nothing about tonight had ended on the terrace. The discovery was the easy part. The easy part had already happened.

Tomorrow she had to hand the lie to someone who trusted her.

setups_to_plant.description: Anya's misleading WISE assessment creates a traceable discrepancy that will be exploited by the mole and contribute to operational failures
Establish Anya's growing guilt about the asymmetry — she negotiates with full access to thoughts while others negotiate blind; she lies while reading the trust that enables the lie

state_deltas_required: Anya must file an assessment to WISE about Damian while managing her conflicting knowledge and loyalties; then she must establish contact
Anya commits institutional betrayal by filing a false report — a traceable lie with unforeseen consequences. Contact with Damian is established. She now operates under two deceptions.
